name: Tension
desc:
Project a psychic field of incredible density and durability.
This often takes the form of a shiny metallic surface, visible only to those with psychic sensitivity.
passive:
Iron Soul:
When you are about to fill your execution talisman and suffer a wound, you can roll 1d6.
On a result of 4 or more, you are 1 stress point below the maximum and ignore any excess, losing the use of this passive until you rest.

powers.name: Aegis
powers.desc:
[Short Snapshot | No pulse cost]
When you or an ally visible within close range of you scores stress from external damage, you can intervene by answering the following questions:
• Can you reach your target in time?
• Is there any part of the environment you can use to protect your target?
• Are you putting your heart into it?

You create an instantaneous tension force of incredible intensity, blocking damage.
For each "yes" answer, roll 1d6.
For each result of 2 or more, reduce the stress suffered by 1, and for each result of 6, reduce it by 2.
This can reduce the stress suffered to 0.
After using this power, it loses its usefulness until you rest.

powers.name: Stasis
powers.desc:
[Damn, 1 scene, Short | Requires 1 pulse]
With one gesture, you can lock yourself or a group of CAT-sized humans or exorcists in a tension cage that covers them like a second skin, paralyzing them.
If a human is hostile or reluctant, roll PSYCHE and expend the psychic pulse only on a success.

Once trapped, your target is immobilized, unable to move or react during the scene, and immune to all damage and effects external.
The effect only ends when the scene ends and it is not possible to dispel it before.
The target can be moved like an object (very rigid) and remains fully conscious while inside, he can see as if he were looking through thick glass and does not need to breathe.

powers.name: Severance
powers.desc:
[Short Snapshot | Requires 1 pulse]
You can project a tensile force of incredible strength onto any edge, as obvious as a blade and as subtle as a fingernail, and use it as a cutting instrument.
Roll PSYCHO to cut an object or opponent up to CAT size with a clean, decisive blow, spending only one psychic pulse on success.
• Gain +1D if attacking to protect someone else
• Gain +1D against immovable objects or opponents.

powers.name: Malleate
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Until rest, Adjacent | Requires 1 pulse]
You can invert and infuse a tension field to make an area of non-living matter incredibly malleable and soft.
The size of this block of matter you can affect is affected by CAT.
Choose one of the following effects and you can gain or grant +1D when you or any ally acts to take advantage of this power:
• Rubber: Matter becomes elastic and flexible.
• Mud: Matter melts and transforms into a thick mud.
Becomes malleable, sticky, and difficult to pass through.
• Liquid: Matter melts and turns into liquid.

This power can easily affect the parameters of rolls, such as difficulty and risk.
When the effect expires, the matter slowly returns to its original state and shape.

powers.name: Fortress
powers.desc:
[Invocation, Until you rest, Short | Requires 1 pulse]
Once per scene, you can create a point voltage field of a given size up to CAT, which appears as a large flat surface of glowing force, invisible to humans.
It can only exist as a straight plane (no curves or bends) and can intercept or overlap any non-living material, but is otherwise as rigid as a solid object and prevents all matter and energy, living or non-living, from traverse.
Has a 2+CAT talisman for its durability, which can take damage as an execution talisman by opponents.
The field lasts until destroyed, until used again, or until restored.

fato:
Tension Users are highly likely to experience episodes of locked-in syndrome at least semi-regularly until they can master their powers.
Consult the field manual.

name: Ardence
desc:
Manipulate potential energy to create flashes of extreme heat or cold.
The most powerful and dangerous of blasphemies — for others and for those who practice it.
passive:
Inner Furnace:
You can acquire an ''Unstable Power'' hook by using any Burning power to increase the power's CAT by up to +2.
When the hook fills, you burn from the inside, sustaining a wound and terminating the hook.
If that wound is fatal, you explode in an area equal to your CAT, annihilating yourself and everything inside in a massive explosion.
Nothing can survive this.

powers.name: Fury
powers.desc:
[Instant, Long | Requires 1 pulse]
You create a fierce blast of destructive energy at a location within range, with a blast area of up to CAT.
When using this power, roll PSYCHE and answer the following questions, gaining +1D for each "yes" answer, expending a psychic pulse only on a success.
• Are you willing to cause indiscriminate damage, regardless of what you destroy, burn, or incinerate?
• Are you willing to let your anger control the outcome?

Any question to which you answer "yes" affects the outcome of the action, regardless of the die result.
Additionally, if you answered "yes" to at least one question, the affected area is ALWAYS equal to the maximum CAT, and allies in the area or near you suffer 2 points of stress, which cannot be ignored.

powers.name: Void
powers.desc:
[Instant, Short | Requires 1 pulse]
You create an instantaneous vacuum by burning the air.
The vacuum generates a deafening roar, affecting an area up to CAT.
Affects everyone in the area except you.
Choose one of the following effects, and then you can gain or grant +1D when you or any ally then takes an action to benefit from this power:
• Weak: Sucks up loose objects that are not attached, worn or bolted.
• Medium: All humans and exorcists in the area are knocked down and pulled toward the center except you.
• Strong: Sins and vehicles up to CAT size are knocked off balance or pulled, depending on their size.
Glass shatters.
The boom is momentarily deafening.

This power can affect dice parameters such as difficulty and risk.

powers.name: Hell
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Adjacent, Until rest | Requires 1 pulse]
You can release energy into the ground and anything that touches you in an area determined by CAT+2, choosing hot or cold.
Choose one of the following effects, which lasts until you rest.
You can gain or grant +1D when you or any ally acts to take advantage of this power:
• Heat: Discomfort for humans, low or high temperature, hot or cold surfaces, etc.
• Scald: Great discomfort for humans, who cannot remain in the area, and discomfort for sinners and exorcists.
Freezes or boils water, pipes, breaks glass, etc.
• Boil: Deadly for humans, sinners and exorcists suffer 2 stress points if they remain in the area for longer than one scene.
Light fires or freeze the air in rooms, melt windows or burn doors, or freeze objects.

This power can affect data parameters such as difficulty and risk.

powers.name: Sabre
powers.desc:
[Instant, CAT Range | Requires 1 pulse]
Release a blast of energy in a highly destructive beam.
The beam travels in a straight line with a range equal to CAT, piercing walls, doors, and obstructions effortlessly.
It is extremely loud and bright.
Roll PSYCHO for its effects, spending only one psychic pulse on a success.

Optionally, you can remove the limiter on this ability when using it.
If the If you do, for every '6' you roll when using the skill, this skill inflicts 1 extra cut on a talisman, but you also suffer 2 stress, which can kill you or cause you an injury.
This stress cannot be reduced or ignored in any way.

powers.name: Storm
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Extreme, All Hunt | No pulse cost]
You can spend any number of psychic pulses to send potential energy into the atmosphere, affecting a microclimate in an area equivalent to CAT+2, with a maximum of CAT 7.
Choose one of the options below, plus one for each psychic pulse spent.
Chosen effects last the entire mission or until dismissed.
• Clear Skies: Clear skies in the area, canceling any weather forecasts (including the below).
• Rain: Rain soaks the area for the duration of the chosen intensity (drizzle, downpour, torrential rain).
• Cold: The air freezes, freezing the water and forming ice on roads and paths.
Any precipitation turns into snow.
• Fog: Dense fog forms, limiting visibility.
• Gale: Strong winds sweep the area, dissipating fog, smoke or dust, making it difficult hearing and movement outdoors.

This power can affect data parameters such as difficulty and risk.
Once used, you lose use of this power until you rest.

fato: The use of Burning would be considered inhumane against human soldiers.

name: Flux
desc: Manipulate the direction and flow of time itself.
passive:
Steal Time:
Once per hunt, you may reroll all of your rest dice for the entire party, treating the second result as final.

powers.name: Reversal
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Instantaneous, Self | Requires 1+ pulse]
You spend up to three psychic pulses to freeze time in an area around you equal to CAT.
Roll 1d6 for each psychic pulse spent and add up the results — that's the number of seconds you have.
Everything that enters that area is immediately affected (including everything that enters or leaves the area), but time flows normally outside of it.
In this frozen time, you are unaffected and:
• You cannot use psychic powers, but you cannot be affected by them either.
Any power currently active on you or affecting you other than this dissipates.
• You can perform an activity or action that fits the duration.
Once you make an action roll for anything, the effect ends, regardless of the result.

Then get temporal instability.

powers.name: Stop
powers.desc:
[Instant, Adjacent | Requires 1 pulse]
You can accelerate the natural healing of your body or the bodies of others, providing the following benefits:
• Immediately heal 1d3 stress on yourself or another target.
If your target is injured, increase this by +1
• You can heal a CAT group of dying or injured humans.
Dying humans are stabilized and are no longer in danger of dying, but become unconscious.
Otherwise, seriously injured humans are healed to the point where they can move (slowly) on their own.
Light wounds are fully healed.

Then get temporal instability.

powers.name: Quickening
powers.desc:
[Adjacent Snapshot]
By touching an object up to CAT in size, you can reverse its passage in time during the last hour.
• This could physically move the object, reverse damage to an object, etc.
• It could still affect the physical world, so anything in the path of an object in a reversal will be hit, and anything placed on it will move with it.

If it deals damage or impact, roll PSYCHE to this.
• Cannot reverse life into non-living matter, such as corpses, but can temporarily move them and reverse the damage as if they were alive.

powers.name: Schism
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Instant, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You can create a bubble of altered time equal to the CAT area.
Within the bubble, you open a window to a day in the past or future from the time the bubble was created.
Gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of this power:
• The state of the area within the bubble is limited to the bubble itself and includes objects or people within it.
• You and your allies can enter or leave the bubble at will.
• Supernatural beings, including exorcists, in the present timeline, who are trapped in the bubble at the time of its creation, will not be affected.
Humans and the world of the present timeline who are trapped in the bubble are "paused", cease to exist for its duration, and have no memory of the incident.
• Objects removed from the bubble, whether from the past or future, including living beings, simply disappear until returned to the bubble. bubble.

The bubble represents an "alternative" timeline, so any changes made within it will not appear in the present or future timeline (i.e. leaving an object in a past timeline will not cause it to appear in the present).

powers.name: Stutter
powers.desc:
[Instant, Short | Requires 1 pulse]
You can briefly reverse time to alter the causality of any event that occurred as a result of an action roll made by you or an ally within CAT range, instantly after seeing the result.
Roll the action again, treating the second result as final.

When using this power, you gain instability temporal.
If you use it again before resting, you gain 1d3 temporal instability.

fato:
This power is relatively new and, as it stands, is little understood.
Fortunately, temporal events are strictly regulated by TEMERITY through a captive Sin called the Spindle.

name: Vector
desc: Imbue objects or living beings with a sudden and intense burst of speed.
passive:
Brake:
Automatically removes the speed of all projectiles that would hit you, causing -1 stress to them.

powers.name: Fling
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Until Rest, Reach CAT+2 | Requires 1 pulse]
You create a weak but persistent vector force in an area that lasts until you rest.
It creates a line about CAT+2 long and a street wide.
This line constantly pushes in one direction (including up or down), like a strong wind.
Allies moving in that direction get +1D actions to move with the current.
Anything that moves against the current current has difficulty, and anything that falls falls slowly.
It becomes difficult for allies to move against the current, and actions taken against anyone trying to fight the current receive +1D.
You can dispel this effect at will.

powers.name: Lift
powers.desc:
[Instantaneous, CAT+1 Range | Requires 1 pulse]
You can infuse strong bursts of airspeed into your fingertips, creating bullets of pressurized air that hit with extreme force.
Roll PSYCHO to gain its effects, expending only one psychic pulse on a success.
• Gain +1D when firing from an elevated position.
• Gain +1D when firing to disarm, distract, or incapacitate rather than deal damage.

powers.name: Current
powers.desc:
[Adjacent, Instant | Requires 1 pulse]
With one touch, you can imbue speed into yourself, another object, or another living being and launch it away.
The combined size of the object or being and the range of the throw must be equal to or less than your CAT+2.
Once thrown, your target's direction cannot be changed.

You can alternatively remove all speed by touching a CAT+2 sized object or person, stopping it completely.
Roll PSYCHE for the effects of this power, including any damage inflicted, and expend a psychic surge only on at least one success.

powers.name: Bullet
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, Self, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You reverse the effect of gravity on yourself and a group of exorcists or CAT-sized humans, with a low but constant vector effect.
Through this scene, all affected receive the following benefits:
• You can run, walk, or climb vertical surfaces.
• You can slow your fall at will and cannot be hurt when falling.
• You can glide for a distance equal to the range CAT.
You need to start at an altitude to get this benefit.

This power can easily affect roll parameters such as difficulty and risk.

powers.name: Finesse
powers.desc:
[All the Hunt, CAT Range | No pulse cost]
Passive: You can precisely manipulate strands of force to perform motor skills that you would perform with your hands at ½ CAT range, such as opening doors, picking up objects, or even typing on a keyboard, etc.
Roll a relevant skill check, such as Interface, to do this.
• You must be able to see your target, even if they are far away.
However, you can perform this manipulation even if your path to the target is blocked, as if you could see it through a window, etc.
• You can pick up objects and move them through the air, but they cannot be larger or heavier than a laptop or a full briefcase.

fato: It has the highest mortality rate among untrained users, who generally die as a result of falls.

name: Gate
desc: Manipulate space like a sculptor works with clay.

powers.name: Tear
powers.desc:
[Invocation, Short, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
By dividing space in creative ways, you create a number of controllable duplicates of any of your limbs or hands equal to CAT+1 on any surfaces within a short distance of you, emerging from a rift in space.
They are stuck in place and cannot move.
Gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of this power:
• You can control them as your normal limbs, performing actions with them and having normal sensations.
• They can be placed on any surface, including moving surfaces or living things.
• You absorb any stress they would suffer from your actions performed through them.

powers.name: Pinch
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Self, CAT Reach, All Hunt | Requires 1 pulse]
You rearrange an area equal to CAT around you, causing human-built structures in the area to rearrange.
Gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to benefit from this power:
• Create or remove doors and windows, or change their existing arrangement.
• Add hallways or rearrange the layout of rooms.
• Change the direction of gravity within a room.
For example, you can make a wall the "floor."
• Increase or decrease the size of a room.
• Rearrange or remove furniture within a room in any way you prefer.

You cannot remove rooms completely, make any room smaller than a closet or larger than a ballroom, or add anything that is not already in a building other than hallways.
This could cause a structure to be larger on the inside than on the outside.
For each choice, the Administrator rolls 1d6.
If he rolls at least a '1', the Administrator gains one use of this power against you in your current CAT and can activate it at any time.

powers.name: Bloom
powers.desc:
[Invocation, Until rest | Requires 1 pulse]
You create a point within CAT range and another point within the same range, although you must be able to see both points when using this ability.
The two points are connected by a rift in the fabric of reality, a portal that can be passed through and connects the two points as if they were side by side.
Objects, beings, and forces up to ½ CAT in size can move freely through the rift for the duration, and the momentum is preserved.

Gain +1D when you or an ally acts to take advantage of this power.

powers.name: Maze
powers.desc:
[Instant, Special Range | Requires 1 pulse]
You can choose a single living being or object that you can see.
The combined size of the object or being and the distance you attempt to move it must be equal to or less than CAT+2.
Roll PSYCHO if your target is resisting, spending only one psychic pulse on a success.
As long as you can see your target, with at least one success, you can compress the space between the two of you to move your target stops right next to you.
From the target's perspective, it doesn't appear to move, but the world distorts around it.
It ignores all physical obstructions between it and you - as long as you can see your target, it simply appears at your side.
You can gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes an action to take advantage of this power.

powers.name: Transmission
powers.desc:
[Instantaneous, CAT+2 Range | Requires 1 pulse]
Move instantly to any area within CAT+2 range.
However, the Administrator will ask the following questions and roll 1d6 for each "no" answer:
• Do you know your destination?
• Can you see where you are going?
• Are you calm and focused?

With at least a '1', you end up in a different location near your destination, but the Administrator chooses where.
With two '1's, you end up somewhere else freely before reaching your final destination.
In addition to being a bit far from your destination, you also suffer 2d3 stress.

fato:
Improper use can lead to extreme personnel losses.
Use with caution.

name: Smother
desc:
Suppress the innate properties of the universe.
Lie to God.
passive:
Absentia:
You can improve the CAT of any of your Choke powers by +2 when you use them, up to a maximum CAT of 7.
However, when you do so, you gain the ''Absence'' Hook.

Hook (Absence):
If this hook fills up, you suffer a wound and briefly pass out.
When you wake up, you will be missing part of the body (roll 1d6).
It simply disappears (A clean wound) as if it had never existed, leaving a stump or hole.
It does not return, even if you heal the wound.
If you have no more body parts to lose (when rolling), reduce the result by 1.
If the result is 0, you lose your head and suffer a (horrifying) instant death.
The lack of body parts can make some rolls difficult or risky, depending on the situation.
You adapt to any disability after the mission, and it has no further effects.
1.
Eye
2.
Nose
3.
Ear
4.
Finger
5.
Toe
6.
Nothing

powers.name: Hollow
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Short, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
With one gesture, you remove the recognizable properties of CAT+1 number of tools, vehicles, windows, doors, or any other distinct objects that can be held or used.
The chosen objects can no longer be used for their original purpose, and no human, sin, or exorcist (including you!) can recognize them — staring at them for too long causes extreme discomfort, even for exorcists.
For example, weapons can no longer be used. shoot, doors can no longer be opened, and you cannot look through windows.

Grant or gain +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of this power.
It can also affect the parameters of rolls while it is active.

powers.name: Abstract
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Short, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You temporarily remove almost all friction from a CAT-sized group of humans or exorcists, or a CAT-sized area.
The area or target(s) becomes incredibly slippery.
If the target is an area, it becomes difficult for anyone to stand, climb, or move normally in it, although people can still slide.
• Roll PSYCHE to affect hostile targets with this power, spending only one psychic pulse on a success.
• You can shape this area if you wish to affect only part of it, or shape a path.

Gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of this power.
This power can also easily affect the parameters of rolls, such as difficulty and risk.

powers.name: Smooth
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, Adjacent, Until rest | Requires 1 pulse]
You temporarily remove the weight of a single object, human or exorcist, making it have a total weight of 450g if heavier.
The size of the object must be CAT or smaller, and you can end this modification at any time, although you may need to use this power again to regain its effects.
Roll PSYCHE for any creative uses of this power, expending a psychic pulse only on a success.
• Gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes an action to take advantage of this power.
• This power ends on the previous target if used again.

This power can easily affect roll parameters such as difficulty and risk.

powers.name: Dark Age
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, Self, Until rest | Requires 1 pulse]
You generate a powerful field from your body, preventing even the simplest human advances from working in the CAT area.
The effect moves with you.
You can choose up to three of the following effects to suppress, ceasing their functioning, and then gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of this power:
• Electricity
• Internet
• Combustion engine
• Running water
• Doorknobs, window latches, zippers, latches
• Open fireplaces

These things stop working even if it doesn't make sense, i.e. stopping the water flow would mean the water pressure would simply stop working.
You can end this effect voluntarily, but you must end all effects at once.

powers.name: Blind
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Adjacent, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
A number of objects or living beings equal to CAT, or an area up to CAT in size that you touch, ceases to produce sound, reflect light, or both in the scene.
Gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of this power:
• When selecting a person, the effect moves with them through the scene.
• When selecting a location, the effect affects an area, removing all sound and/or light.
You can "filter" this effect by allowing light or sound within the location to function normally, but preventing them from entering or leaving the area.

This power can easily affect roll parameters such as difficulty and risk.

fato: The prospects for long-term survival are poor, but are offset by usefulness; therefore, users of this blasphemy are good candidates for organ transplantation (see TM ref 4456).

name: Whisper
desc:
Her shadow is lively and hungry.
She knows the future.
passive:
Shadow:
You harbor a separate being that follows you everywhere, even when you sleep.

THE SHADOW is intangible and invisible to all, even those with psychic sensitivity.
It can only weakly interact with the physical world and has a mind and senses of its own.
Its range is short range from you.
It can pass through walls and surfaces easily, but retracts into your body when exposed to bright light, preventing her from doing anything.

You can communicate with her telepathically, but this is dangerous and causes 1 point of stress after any interaction ends.
She is under no obligation to tell you the truth unless you use her powers.
You can communicate with her safely using her abilities, and she knows the future.
The Administrator will answer for her.

powers.name: Omen
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, Self, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
When searching for a human, sin, exorcist, location, or object, you can declare "I feel a chill."
You send a psychic pulse in a CAT range around you, which remains active during this scene.
As long as your target is close enough to be within reach, you feel a strong sensation of cold and discomfort.
You can pinpoint this sensation easily.
Tracking your target is never difficult while this power is active.
If your target is within close range of you, you also gain +1D on any checks to track or locate them.

powers.name: Shiver
powers.desc:
[Instant, CAT Range | Requires 1 pulse]
Examine a human or exorcist you can see within CAT range, roll PSYCHE and ask their shadow one of the following questions, plus one more for each success.
She answers truthfully, but can use a maximum of three words for each answer:
• Is this person lying?
• What is the main emotion this person is feeling?
• Where did this person just come from?
• Where did they plan to go next?

Gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of each response.

powers.name: Dissect
powers.desc:
[Instantaneous, CAT+2 Range | No pulse cost]
When an ally is on the scene, you are not present in that scene and your ally is within range of CAT+2, you can use this power to enter the scene, having already predicted that this course of events would occur.
Roll PSYCHE and choose one of the following options for each success:
• No one is following you
• You are hidden
• You can enter the location your ally is in without being distracted or damaged
• You have I get a useful tool or object for the current situation (a key, a gun, a wrench, etc.)

After using this power, you will lose your ability to use it until you rest.

powers.name: Precognition
powers.desc:
[Instant, Self | Requires 1 pulse]
When the Administrator is describing a scene or you are about to take an action, you may "flashback."
Take an action check or replay this scene in the past where you had a vision of the present moment.
This cannot completely alter the established facts of the present (you cannot have knocked out someone in the past if you just talked to them in the present, for example), but it can change the situation or alter details in the present, or it can help you or anyone ally.

For example, you could have made preparations for the current moment (locked or unlocked a door, put away some equipment, made a phone call, etc.).
If you use this power for equipment, mark the use of PK as normal.
If the situation is complicated, also suffer 1 point of non-lethal stress.
If it is complex or unlikely, suffer 3 points of non-lethal stress.

powers.name: Omnipresence
powers.desc:
[Instant, Self | Requires 1 pulse]
Ask your shadow: "What will happen if I do action if you want, but if you take the same course of action in the future, use the pre-rolled risk die.

fato:
This alter ego usually manifests at puberty and only you can see it, remaining invisible even to other exorcists.
Common belief is that the "shadow" seen is a component of your own future death.

name: Edit
desc:
Things as they are do not have to be as they are.
You can choose, filtering the threads of possibilities and inviting them to merge with the real world.
passive:
Mimic:
You can change small details of your appearance.
You can modify any of the following aspects of your resting body, within a variation of your original body:
• Physical characteristics, such as height and weight
• Aesthetic characteristics, such as facial features, skin color, hair, and gender presentation
• Age, from 13 to 88 years
You always have a vaguely similar appearance to yourself, like a relative distant.
Your clothes always change to fit you, although you cannot alter them.
It does not alter your general abilities or capabilities in any way, nor does it restore lost body parts, nor hide marks of sin or scars.

powers.name: Uniform
powers.desc:
[See you rest, Curto | Requires 1 pulse]
When you need any common object, tool, or vehicle that fits in a small room, you can make it appear on a surface within reach as if it had always been there, without spending KP.
However, the administrator chooses one, or two if the item is dangerous or rare:
• The item is used, dented, scratched, or is of low quality.
• The item is a real item that someone nearby owned that has gone missing, and that person will come look for it.
• The item is defective in some way and looks like a cheap imitation made of strange materials with a spongy or organic texture.
• The item is missing parts and does not work as well as it should with them.

The item cannot be unique, such as a car, key, book, etc.
specific; it is a generic representative of a category.
It disappears after a period of inactivity.

powers.name: Absurd
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, Self, Until rest | Requires 1 Pulse]
You do a brief edit of yourself.
You can't do this in public (you need privacy, however tenuous it may be).
This power makes you officially part of any profession or group with more than 5 members, with whatever uniform, equipment, ID card, membership, etc.
necessary, and alters reality for this to happen.
Even if people don't particularly remember you being part of one group, they may have a vague notion that you were a member.

You do not gain any specific abilities, and any changes you may have to make will be personal and must include things you could wear or carry in one or both hands.

Gain or grant +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of this power.

powers.name: Utility
powers.desc:
[Curse, Short, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You exchange a number of humans' or exorcists' CAT for a different version of themselves from an alternate timeline.
You must roll PSYCHE for this power to take effect on hostile targets, spending only one psychic pulse on a success.
This can change:
• What the target is wearing but not holding (so whatever they are holding in their hand remains the same)
• Physical appearance of targets, according to your Mimic power.

Targets retain their memories, and it does not alter reality to accommodate the change, and can easily disorient unprepared humans.
The change is perfect in all other respects.
Grant or receive +1D when you or any ally takes the next action to take advantage of this power.

powers.name: Copy
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Adjacent, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You create a strong field that affects all matter in an area approximately the size of a small room, which must contain you.
In this area, you gain +1D to examine its contents and:
• Can make any matter transparent or opaque.
• Can change the lighting of the room as if it were illuminated by an invisible light source, or extinguish any light sources in the room.
• Can move any objects in the area without touching them and "pin" them to any point in space, causing them to float.
• Can safely disassemble any loose inanimate object or furniture into its constituent parts or reassemble any broken object, as long as the pieces are present.

This effect expires when the scene ends or if you leave the area for any reason.

powers.name: Filter
powers.desc:
[Summon, adjacent, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You create a temporary, exact copy of a human or exorcist.
• This creates a doppelgänger: a simple, obedient clone without much intelligence or ability to speak.
• You can give him simple one- or two-sentence instructions, which he will follow to the best of his ability.
• He dissolves into a pale goo when the scene ends, when touched by anyone other than you, or if he suffers any damage.

This ability creates a copy of the target in its current state, including any mundane objects the person is carrying or wearing.
A doppelgänger cannot obtain, use, or benefit from psychic powers and only rolls 1d6 to perform any action.

fato: The CAIN doctrine clearly states that the existence of "alternative" realities has not currently been proven.

name: Bind
desc: With the will of those who possess it, even Sins can be controlled, linked to talismans specially prepared in spiritual form.
passive:
Sin Binding:
You have the forbidden ability to bind Sins. Bound has animalistic form and abilities - you can determine which form he takes.
He can understand language, but cannot speak, and is invisible to humans.

He can follow you at close range, obey simple orders, and use his abilities to do anything.
His general abilities are CAT 0.

If he suffers any stress, he will be banished for the rest of the scene; however, you can psychically absorb all the stress he suffers to avoid this effect.

In a conflict scene, you can sacrifice your ability to act on your turn to allow your sin to act in your place, giving you commands.
Otherwise, he does not act independently in these scenes.

powers.name: Forbidden Spirit
powers.desc:
[Self, Snapshot | Requires 1 pulse]
You can spend a Psychic Pulse to empower your sin in a single action.
As part of this action:
• After this scene, he can reduce any stress suffered by 1 (minimum 0).
This effect stacks if this power is used again.
• You remove the limitations of your spirit.
The action gains +1D and causes it to undergo a monstrous transformation similar to its form
He becomes equal in size to CAT+1 and can easily move, lift, strike, or throw objects or beings of equal size.
Roll PSYCHE for its effects.
After the action, he returns to his normal size.

powers.name: Surrender
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, Self, Until rest | Requires 1 pulse]
You use the energy of your sin to partially merge with your essence.
Your body mutates slightly to adapt to this change.
Immediately manifest a temporary sin mark and roll to determine the location and ability, which you retain until you end this ability early or rest.
You can gain 1 sin to reroll the mark ability as many times as you want at any time while this power is active.
If you shed sin while If this effect is active and successfully resisted, you will manifest the chosen mark of sin permanently rather than rolling dice to obtain it.

powers.name: Horde Spirit
powers.desc:
[Self, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You can spend a Psychic Pulse to strengthen your sin for one scene.
The next time it uses an action to shift or move, it gains +1D.
As part of this action:
• You can transform it into the form of a vehicle or rideable creature up to CAT size for one scene.
It can move at CAT speed, becomes partially visible to humans, and has room for a group of human passengers or ½ CAT-sized exorcists.
• He can glide for a short distance while in vehicle form, with or without passengers.

powers.name: Hunter Spirit
powers.desc:
[Self, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You can spend a Psychic Pulse to strengthen your sin for one scene.
The next time he uses a track or observe action, he gains +1D.
As part of this action:
• He can now separate from you at extreme distances when released.
• You can communicate telepathically with him.
• He gains the ability to fly, see and smell extremely well - he can also see in the dark and in thermal spectrum, with clarity over long distances.
• You can concentrate, dissociating yourself from your body and making yourself extremely vulnerable.
While concentrating in this way, however, you can use your sin's senses instead of your own.
You can activate and deactivate this effect at will during the scene.

powers.name: Penumbra
powers.desc:
[Invocation, Adjacent, Until rest | Requires 1 pulse]
You create an area drawn like a large circle, encompassing an area up to size CAT+1, and choose a type from the list below.
The prison takes only a moment to create, but takes a few minutes to activate and for its effects to take effect.
It lasts until you rest or until used again.
• A white cloak prevents the Sins from entering, but allows humans and exorcists to pass through normally.
• A black cloak prevents entry by humans or exorcists.
Additionally, humans will (generally) not be able to see into the area and will act as if it does not exist.
• You can reverse this effect if you wish, preventing exit rather than entry.

The prison can be destroyed by a determined supernatural attacker, but has a CAT 4+ talisman for durability, resisting stress like an execution talisman.

fato:
The practice of binding is an ancient but heretical art, punishable by immediate execution.
Fortunately, CAIN offers an indefinite sentence to binders who remain in its service.

name: Jaunt
desc: Cut the body and soul with a carving knife.
passive:
Ghostwire:
You can connect your mind telepathically with a number of willing people you touch equal to CAT.
As long as you are a long distance away from each other, you can talk telepathically and sense each other's emotional state.
This effect lasts until you use it again, until someone becomes unconscious, or until you or someone else ends the connection.

powers.name: Possession
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, Self, Until rest | Requires 1 pulse]
You can sense the invisible world of the traces of grace.
When using this power, you close your eyes and can see, through your eyelids, the patterns that the soul leaves in the environment.
• You gain the ability to see living beings, even through walls, within CAT range.
• You can see the traces of a sin or someone strong in grace, like an exorcist, who leaves a faint trail of light through the air.
• You gain +1D on actions to track or locate living beings or traces of sin in the area.
However, you cannot see any inanimate matter (you are effectively blind) while you maintain this power, and performing actions that rely on sight are difficult.

This power ends when you open your eyes or when you rest.

powers.name: Geist
powers.desc:
[Adjacent, Instant | No pulse cost]
You can inject a semblance of life into the corpses of up to CAT humans or exorcists by touching them in the eyes.
You can ask the corpse(s) three questions in total (regardless of how many you animate), after the effect ends and they return to death.
The effect also expires if an hour passes.
• You cannot use this power on the same corpse more than once.
• The power does not return
If the head or brain are missing, or if the body has no tongue, etc., this power will not work.

A corpse is required to respond truthfully, but can only speak about the knowledge and memories it had before dying.
It may only have partial knowledge of a situation, or it may speak from its own point of view.

Then lose use of this power until rested.

powers.name: Threads
powers.desc:
[Curse, Extreme range, 1 scene | Requires 1 Pulse]
You choose a group of willing people, ½ CAT or less in extreme range, who should be able to hear (even telepathically) or see you.
You absorb their psychic presence into your body during this time.
Their bodies become numb, vulnerable, and insensitive.
However:
• They now share control of your body with you, including all senses.
• You can cede control of your body to them to allow them to make action checks using your skills or abilities, but your body (equipment, access, etc.) will not be affected.
• You can normally assist or assist them in these skills.

They cannot use psychic powers while possessing you in this way, and you will suffer the damage or consequences of their actions.

powers.name: Desecrate
powers.desc:
[Self, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You can project your perception outside your body and wander within a range of CAT+2, becoming a being made of pure psychic energy.
• When practicing this power, your real body becomes insensible and defenseless.
• You can fly at CAT speed, are invisible to those who are not psychically sensitive, and can pass through walls, floors, and objects easily while in this form.
• You cannot interact with the
You cannot use or benefit from your own psychic powers, but psychic powers or the effects of others can still affect you.

If your form is destroyed in some way (by a supernatural force), you suffer 1 point of stress, this power ends, and you cannot use it again until the scene passes.
This power can easily affect the parameters of rolls, such as difficulty and risk.

powers.name: Passenger
powers.desc:
[Curse, Short, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You can project your perception outside your body to possess a human, animal, or corpse (in good condition) that you can see within range for one scene.
Supernatural beings are immune to this effect.
Humans who attempt to resist may need a PSYCHE roll to be successfully possessed.
• When possessing another being, their real body becomes insensitive and defenseless.
• For humans and animals, You cannot force a target to harm itself or to take actions that indirectly cause it to suffer harm.
• The actions the target takes use your skills, but the target's body or equipment can alter the circumstances.

You are expelled from the body if it is stressed.

Gain +1D on your next action after using this power.

fato:
Among all exorcists, Haunt users are the most likely to "empty themselves" in their sleep, leaving an empty body.
This event is very rare, its cause is unknown, and it is 100% fatal.

name: Palace
desc: The contents of your mind are as concrete to you as reality itself.
passive:
Sanctum:
You and allies you rest with can enter your psychic palace while resting.
This improves the rest checks of you and an ally of your choice who is resting with you by +1.

• The palace is a mental projection, a dream space that takes the form of a large house, residence, or mansion in a location of your choosing.
Leaving the location simply returns to you, and as a phenomenon Purely psychic, you can control its appearance and decoration.
• Taking damage in a palace instantly expels the person from there, awakening them, rather than causing them actual harm.
Visitors can leave at any time.
• Mentally entering the palace requires only closing your eyes and concentrating, leaving your outer body defenseless and unresponsive.
This can be done by you at any time, and by your allies resting with you or your powers.

powers.name: Library
powers.desc:
[Invocation | No pulse cost]
Passive: Your palace has a tulpa, a psychic being that takes the form of a servant or butler.
He is loyal to you, and you can determine his personality and appearance by acquiring this power.

Active: You summon your tulpa, choosing one:
• Have your tulpa assist you in a research, crafting, or investigation-related task, granting +1D to your next roll and making an extra cut into a talisman for every '6' you roll.
• Briefly manifest your tulpa outside your palace, at short range, like a real person, a mirror of your appearance inside the palace, but dressed however you want.
They have roughly the capabilities of an average person (category 0) and roll 2d for activities a typical servant or butler might do and 0d for everything else.
Any damage suffered by them banishes them back to the palace.

powers.name: Cellar
powers.desc:
[1 scene, Investigation area | Requires 1 pulse]
Choose a person or group of up to a CAT in the investigation area and say their (real) name(s) out loud.
You can bring yourself and their psychic shadow(s) into your palace no matter where they are.
If your target(s) is willing, you can choose to bring her/their real psychic consciousness into your palace, causing her/him to perceive and remember what is happening inside, as if she/he were in a dream.
Her real body is left unconscious and vulnerable. but for reluctant targets you need to roll PSYCHE and spend the psychic pulse only on success.
She(s) is not affected, but the double is a copy of her mind at the time of the summon.
Any memories formed by the double will not be transferred.
The double cannot leave until the scene ends or until she(s) takes damage as usual.
A person or duplicate Summoned in this way is in no way required to behave differently from its original version.

Grant or gain +1D on the next roll, whether it is yourself or an ally who is benefiting from this power.

powers.name: Foyer
powers.desc:
[Self, 1 scene | No pulse cost]
Once per scene, you can open any closed door and find yourself in a bar (physical and real) instead of the room you'd expect.
It's a small, typical bar, well-stocked, with snacks, some hot dishes, and alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks.
It's restocked between missions.
The bar only exists while you're inside it or holding the door; external items or people inside it are pushed out before it disappears.

When resting in your bar, you can roll 1d3+1 and do one of the following actions, expending charges from the die you just rolled for each option:
• 1 charge: Removes 1 stress from a person
• 2 charges: Clears 1 mark on all of a person's hooks
• 3 charges: Removes one wound

For each, Describe a drink, snack, meal, or other form of relaxation or wellness that you are preparing for your guest.

powers.name: Parlor
powers.desc:
[Instant, Self | No pulse cost]
Your palace has a library of psychic subconscious information.
When you wish to gather information or investigate any matter, you can get a +1D roll when accessing this library.
However, the Administrator then rolls 1d6 for each of the following:
• Is the information rare?
• Is the information prohibited in any way?
• Is the information pertinent to a group powerful?

For every '1', you suffer 2 non-lethal stress when reading something disturbing in the library.

powers.name: Bar
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, infinite range, Instant | Requires 1 pulse]
You can simulate situations within your palace before putting them into practice in reality.
You can use this power and roll dice to set a number of allies equal to ½ your CAT, even if you are not physically present.
However, you can only set a target if you can describe the way you have trained or prepared with it, or with a psychic copy of it, for the current situation.
This setup can never be risky, but it cannot reduce the risk.
If you fail the configuration roll, you may take 1d3 nonlethal stress to roll again, treating the second result as final.

name: Sympathy
desc:
Humans leave impressions on everything they touch.
You can do more than touch.
passive:
Resonance:
At the start of the quest, roll on the resonance table.
When making this roll, roll 1d3, then 1d6, then check the resonance tables after this blasphemy entry.
When you are making an action roll and are using an item with which you resonate, you receive a +1D bonus.
You can spend a psychic pulse at any time to roll a additional resonance.
You can keep up to three resonances active simultaneously and can only benefit from one at a time.

Resonance Table (1d3 + 1d6):
• 11: Cellphones
• 12: Lights
• 13: Knives
• 14: Keys
• 15: Books
• 16: Clubs baseball
• 21: Balls
• 22: Guns
• 23: Mugs
• 24: Computers
• 25: Shoes
• 26: Power tools
• 31: Ropes
• 32: Hammers
• 33: Cars
• 34: Doors
• 35: Backpacks
• 36: Gloves

powers.name: Amplify
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
For the scene, you can create an incredibly strong bond with an object you are holding in one or both hands.
• You are now in resonance with this item.
It is still mundane.
• You can now use it as a mundane slashing or bludgeoning weapon, even though it would not normally be a weapon.
It has approximately the power of a CAT 0 staff or sword.
• The item becomes virtually indestructible and you can have it return to your hand, flying through the air at a short distance.
• You can discharge this power to strike the object, granting it supernatural destructive power equal to CAT.
Roll PSYCHE for its effects (it gains +1D on the roll as usual due to resonance).
Then end this effect and destroy the item.

powers.name: Bond
powers.desc:
[Instant, Adjacent | No pulse cost]
You can touch objects to remotely view their memories.
You can view a number of days equal to your CAT.
Roll PSYCHE and then ask a question, plus an additional question for each success.
• Where has this object been?
• Who touched this object?
• What was this object used for?
• What else is this object connected to?
The memories of an object are impressionistic and imprecise, and are generally only "aware" of their immediate surroundings.
After using this power, lose its use until you rest.

powers.name: Psychometry
powers.desc:
[Summon, Adjacent, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
You can expand the mundane properties of a common non-weapon item to extreme levels.
When you touch a mundane object up to the size of a CAT, you automatically resonate with it in the scene, and its properties are magnified to extreme levels, as if it were on the scale of a CAT.
For example:
• The speed, handling, and endurance of a car
• The brightness and intensity of a light, and the area it illuminates
• A door's ability to lock and resist impacts

This can easily affect the difficulty and risk of rolls.
The object remains mundane.

powers.name: Diplomacy
powers.desc:
[Instant, Short | Requires 1 pulse]
You make a simple request to an object as if it were a person, or ask it a simple yes or no question.
For example, you can ask a door to open or keep closed (even if it normally couldn't be locked, or you don't have the key), a computer to turn off or find information for you, or a car to start without a key or drive itself.
If you need to make a test for this, roll PSYCHE or use a social action such as negotiation or authority.
You can affect objects up to CAT size with this.
Objects that are asked questions can only respond with yes or no and cannot vocalize.

powers.name: Alliance
powers.desc:
[Invocation, Short, 1 scene | Requires 1 pulse]
An object up to CAT size at short range can now perform a help action on an ally, rolling 1d6, or PSYCHE if you are in resonance with that object.
The object can suffer or cause consequences normally from these actions.
Allies must be able to interact with it or use the object to gain its benefits.
The object does not gain the ability to move or animate in any way, but fortune simply bends to your around.

fato: PSYCHOMETRY IS PROHIBITED ON CAMPUS - CASTLE REF 0094

name: Law
desc:
A force so primitive it rewrites reality.
You don't bend the laws — you edit them.

powers.name: Law
powers.desc:
[Transmute, Adjacent, 1 Scene | Spend all remaining psyche bursts — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
You set a rule of physical reality that affects everything in a circular area around you, called the Court, with a radius equal to CAT.
Any changes made take place instantly and persist inside the area, but the area itself does not move.

This power can always eliminate, destroy, or totally change mundane beings, objects or structures.
Strong willed or supernatural beings such as exorcists or sins can partly resist its effects — but humans are always affected instantly.
If you exit the area or the scene ends, the effect ends.

To use Law, spend all your remaining psyche bursts (min 1) and fill in the sentence:
"In the Court, _____ is/are _____"
You can only use words from the Terms of Law list below.
Additional effects are up to the GM and can be improvised depending on the fiction.

Then, choose one effect:
• Grant up to three advantage dice (total) to self or an ally while the court is active. These can be given out one at a time or all at once.
• Instantly kill all humans in the area or that enter the area while the court is active.
• Slash a talisman by rolling PSYCHE. This deals +1 slash on success. This can be performed when first using Law, then once more again while it is active.
• Make something specific less hard, or less risky while the court is active.

This power may easily affect the parameters of rolls while in the area, and may make it so rolls are not even required for certain tasks.

TERMS OF LAW:
1. Fast   2. Slow   3. Sticks   4. Stones   5. Paper   6. Cloth   7. Blood   8. Nails   9. Skin   10. Slings or bullets   11. Arrows   12. Blades   13. Solid   14. Liquid   15. Metal   16. Soft   17. Sharp   18. Heavy   19. Light   20. Earth   21. Fire   22. Water   23. Air   24. Forbidden   25. Pulled   26. Repulsed

fato:
High Blasphemy — gained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP cap or reduce the sin overflow cap.

name: Null
desc:
The absolute negation of all psychic interference.
You don't resist the power — you simply don't exist to it.

powers.name: Null
powers.desc:
[Instant, Charm, 1 Scene | Spend all remaining psyche bursts — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
For the duration of the scene, you become completely immune to psychic phenomena.
Anything inflicting physical harm — like the physical attacks of Sins — can affect you normally.
For example, you could be harmed by a thrown piece of furniture or a bullet, but not by a blast of purely psychic energy or a form of mind control.

You cannot be affected by afflictions (though they still remain on you, their abilities are merely suppressed for the scene).

In return, for the duration, you cannot use or be affected by any blasphemy other than this one — including those from your allies — and any currently affecting you end.

Unlike other powers, you cannot end this effect early.

powers.name: Immaculate Defiance of Heaven
powers.desc:
[Permanent]
This power has the same function as Null, except it is permanent. It has the following effects:

• You can no longer use or be affected by blasphemies.
• Your sin resets to 0. You no longer gain or use sin or can enter sin overflow for any reason.
• You can now spend 1d3 stress instead of a psyche burst to gain +1D on any action by using your powerful physical abilities.
• Fighting sins with mundane abilities is no longer hard. In addition, your mundane physical abilities are about 1/2 CAT in capability.
• You gain +1 max injury.
• Your natural lifespan extends by 10d10 years.

fato:
High Blasphemy — gained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP cap or reduce the sin overflow cap.

name: Entwine
desc:
You entangle your mind with another's in a loving embrace.
Distance does not exist between entwined souls.

powers.name: Entwine
powers.desc:
[Instant, 1 Mission | No psyche burst cost]
Choose another willing exorcist. You entangle your mind with theirs in a loving embrace.
For the duration of the mission, both gain the following effects:

• When one of you gains or loses stress, or spends or gains a psyche burst, you may spend or assign it to either person, no matter the distance.
Harm, stress, etc., supernaturally appears on each other's bodies.
Anything transferred like this cannot be reduced in any way.
• You can telepathically communicate with that person over extreme distance. You also feel any strong emotion they feel reflexively, even involuntarily.

However:
• When one of you gains an affliction or hook, both of you gain that affliction or hook.
• When one of you gains sin, you both gain that amount of sin.
• If either of you suffer sin overflow or instant death, this blasphemy breaks for the rest of the mission. It cannot be ended otherwise.

After the mission — even if this power broke early:
• Both of your physical appearances change to match the other (your eye or hair color, age, gender, height, body or face shape, etc.).
Each of you chooses a feature to share with the other.
The process takes about three days and is unpleasant.

* Prolonged use of this power over time will lead to a physical and mental merge that is described by Charity as the purest form of bliss. This has not been verified by Temerity.

fato:
High Blasphemy — gained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP cap or reduce the sin overflow cap.

name: Strength
desc:
A rare mutation of the Amplify blasphemy.
Without proper training, using this blasphemy will cause internal organs to violently burst from the body.

powers.name: Strength
powers.desc:
[Charm, 1 Scene | Spend all remaining psyche bursts — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
Your mundane physical abilities are now equal to CAT and fighting sins with mundane forces is no longer hard.

• You can push any physical action to CAT+2 instead by gaining 2d3 stress. This could inflict an injury.
• All weapons you use break after use unless specifically tempered. You can temper any weapon between missions by spending 1 scrip per weapon.
• At the end of the scene, you take an injury and become comatose until your group rests. You recover at the start of the rest period and can otherwise rest normally.

fato:
High Blasphemy — gained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP cap or reduce the sin overflow cap.

name: Shake
desc: You enforce a game upon all those around you, and reality bends to ensure the rules are followed.

powers.name: Shake
powers.desc:
[Transmute, Short, 1 Scene | Spend all remaining psyche bursts — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
You may spend all your remaining psyche bursts to instantly enforce a game from the list below on yourself and all other humans, sins, or exorcists in range determined by CAT. Breaking the rules of the game causes excruciating pain, hemorrhaging, and eventually death. As long as the rule applies:

• For you and allies, actions become harder when you must avoid breaking the rule with your actions
• If through your actions you or an ally intentionally breaks the rule, you take 2d3 stress, which cannot be reduced in any way and could inflict injuries as normal.

In return:
• If the target, through their actions, breaks a rule intentionally, they take 1d3 slashes on their execution talisman. Humans are killed instantly. They are aware of this effect.
• You may set up an ally for free, three times, while the rule is active, and without rolling. Describe how you are taking advantage of the rule. Any normal effects of setup apply to these special setup actions, and you can take them without counting as an action in a conflict scene.

You cannot end this effect early, and it lasts for the scene.

GAMES:
• The Floor is Lava (Don't Touch the Ground).
• King of the Hill (Must stay inside a small area).
• Reverse Tag (Don't touch a sin, human, or exorcist).
• Look No Hands (Don't Use Your Hands).
• No Talking Contest (Don't speak or make loud noises).
• Marco Polo (Don't open eyes. Targets must say 'Polo' when you say 'Marco' and cannot stray from short range from you).
• Red Light Green Light (Those affected by the rule see a phantom red and green light and are forced to stop moving on red light).

fato:
High Blasphemy — gained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP cap or reduce the sin overflow cap.
